title: Ash and Bone
prompt: Wide shot, ash-grey plain under cracked violet-black sky. A column of fifty thousand refugees. At the lead, Kael carries a wooden litter bearing white-haired Selene. Beside them walks Mira, eyes fixed on the air. Distant, a massive, cold Gate Stone glows faintly.
narrative: The column moves in silence. Kael’s shoulders brace against the litter’s weight. Selene’s silver eyes are open, fixed on the bruised sky. Mira’s hand trembles as she feels the Weave fray. The Gate Stone pulses with a cold, blue light on the horizon.
narration: The ground is warm. It breathes. Ash swirls in the stale air, tasting of ozone and endings. Fifty thousand ghosts walk toward a stone that may not answer. At the front, the elder lies upon her wooden throne, her breath shallow, her sight turned inward. Her grandson’s healer’s hands are white-knuckled on the poles. The rift-walker beside them does not look at the stone. She looks at the threads between things, feeling them snap, one by one. The song is already in Selene’s throat, a cold weight waiting. The world holds its breath.

title: The Weight of Her
prompt: Wide shot from behind. Kael's spine is a bowed arch under the wooden litter. Selene is a still, shrouded shape. Mira walks beside him, her gaze fixed on unseen threads. The cracked grey earth stretches toward a horizon of pale violet sky. The refugee column is a slow, dark scar.
narrative: Kael's hands are a map of his journey. Mira's silence is a deliberate thing. She watches the warp and weft of possibility, not the suffering of the present. The weight is Kael's to bear alone.
narration: On the third day, the pine wood bites back. Kael's palms are raw meat against the sinew bindings. Each step grinds stone dust into the weeping blisters. Selene is a weight of breath and bone, lighter than a ghost. Her fungal-fiber robes absorb no sound. Mira walks in the litter's shadow, two paces left. Her eyes are on the air itself, tracing threads only she sees. Eight futures unspool in her vision. In seven, a song becomes a scream becomes light. In one, the light does not stop. She does not know which ending is the greater horror.

title: The Gate Stone
prompt: Medium shot. Selene lies motionless on the wooden litter, silver eyes fixed on the massive, frost-covered monolith ahead. Kael’s knuckles are white on the litter poles. Mira crouches, pinching a fraying thread of pale light. The black sky presses down.
narrative: The monolith dominates the dead valley a mile away. Mira touches the Weave and recoils. The connection is fragile, dangerously thin.
narration: The Gate Stone cleaves the valley. It is taller than any ancient tree, a slab of obsidian darkness veined with frost. The air shimmers with impossible heat, yet cold radiates from the stone’s skin. Mira halts. Her fingers close on nothing, then pull. A gossamer thread appears, stretched to translucence. The Weave here isn’t just thin; it is about to unravel. She has seen this fraying in her visions. Kael lowers the litter. He does not speak. Selene’s gaze never leaves the stone. Her lips part, a silent, dry whisper before the song begins.

title: Eight Futures
prompt: Over-the-shoulder shot from Kael's shoulder, Mira facing them with dark circles under her haunted eyes and cropped dark hair wind-tossed, Selene visible on the litter in the midground, the Gate Stone looming black against a violet sky, frost visible on the monolith's surface.
narrative: Mira tells Kael what she has seen. Eight possible futures. In seven, the song kills Selene. In one, the Gate does not open at all. There is no future where everyone lives.
narration: Mira faces Kael across the litter. Her eyes are ringed with grey. She has not slept. She tells him what she has seen. Eight threads. Eight possible outcomes. In seven of them, Selene opens her mouth and sings and the Gate Stone splits open and light pours through and the refugees walk into a world that still has air and water and soil. And in those seven, Selene dies. Her throat tears. Her lungs collapse. Three hundred years of breath spent in a single song. Kael does not look away. His jaw sets. His healer's hands curl into fists at his sides. The eighth thread is worse. In the eighth, the Gate does not open at all.

title: He Refuses, The Stone Waits
prompt: Low angle: Kael's clenched fists white-knuckled over the litter's edge. Selene's silver eyes stare up from the fungal-fiber robes, her white hair a wild halo. Mira stands apart, back turned to them, facing the dark, cold Gate Stone.
narrative: Kael refuses to carry his grandmother to her death. Selene's voice, unused for days, cuts through the air like grinding gravel, compelling him forward.
narration: Kael lowers the litter. The wood groans and splinters against the frozen ground. He stands over her, a trembling pillar of refusal. "No." The word is a shard in his throat. Selene's silver eyes, vast as forgotten skies, hold him. Her voice tears free, dry as ancient dust. "There is no one else." Behind them, the refugees' murmurs are a tide against a dying shore. Her words are stones dropping into silence. Fifty thousand souls. A collapsing world. No other path. Kael's hands shake—hands that set bones, that soothed fevers. Now made to carry life toward its ending. Mira's gaze is fixed on the Stone. The threads of fate around her do not shimmer, do not shift. They are still.

title: The First Song
prompt: Close-up of Selene’s ancient face, deep grooves in skin like cracked clay, silver eyes glowing faintly. White hair wisps against rough fungal-fiber robes. Dying sky’s pale light highlights her weathered human features and the slight tremble of her lips.
narrative: Selene’s memory surfaces—a child beneath a waterfall, where the Weave first sang in her bones. She carries that song still, knowing every pitch of the dying world. The knowledge is etched in her as the song begins.
narration: Selene’s eyes shut. When they open, the silver burns. She speaks of a waterfall from her seventh year—her mother holding her beneath it. The water didn’t speak; it resonated. The frequency hummed in her teeth, vibrated the bones of her skull. She felt it in her marrow. That was two hundred and ninety-three years ago. She has listened since. Every living thing, every stone, has its pitch. The Gate Stone’s is cold, sharp as shattered ice. The world’s pitch is low, a grinding groan like a tooth wrenched from its socket. She knows them all. The song is not learned. It is her blood’s memory. Kael kneels in the dirt. His fists loosen. He nods once, a sharp jerk of his chin.

title: The Last Mile
prompt: Wide shot, Kael lifting the wooden litter onto his blistered shoulders with Selene upon it, Mira walking ahead pointing toward the towering black monolith of the Gate Stone, the three figures small against cracked ashen ground.
narrative: Kael heaves the litter. His blistered hands find their grooves in the pine. He does not look at Selene. He looks at the Gate Stone. Mira leads, moving like someone threading a needle in a storm. The Weave here is a physical pressure, tightening around them all.
narration: The wood groans as Kael lifts. His palms are raw meat against the splintered pine, the pain a distant fire. He does not look at his grandmother. He looks at the stone. One mile. Maybe less. Mira moves ahead. She feels the threads now, not as visions, but as a fine mesh vibrating against her skin, a net pulling tight. One wrong breath and something severs. Behind them, the refugees are frozen. Fifty thousand souls silent in the ash, a held breath of humanity watching three walk toward a millennial silence. The wind carries the scent of sulfur and old blood, and now, the sharp, electric smell of ozone.

title: Cold to the Touch
prompt: High angle looking down at Kael setting the litter at the base of the Gate Stone, Mira kneeling beside Selene with one hand on the old woman's forehead, the dark monolith towering above them, frost forming on the stone's surface and on Kael's palms, violet sky beyond.
narrative: They reach the Gate Stone. Frost blooms where Kael touches it. He sets the litter at its base. Mira places her hand on Selene's forehead and reads the thread one last time.
narration: The Gate Stone is a slab of night, rime-frosted and whispering cold. Kael’s palms burn white where they grip the litter’s handles; the frost bites his skin as he sets it down. Selene lies still upon the woven fungal fibers. Her breath, a winter ghost. Mira kneels, her hand resting on the old woman’s forehead. The skin is furnace-hot. She reads the threads—seven fray into nothing, one congeals into silence. The body knows. Mira’s hand trembles. She pulls it back, the heat lingering on her fingers like a curse.

title: She Opens Her Mouth
prompt: Extreme close-up, Selene's mouth opening in a silent scream, silver light pouring from her throat. Kael's scarred hand grips the litter edge, knuckles white. Mira's wide eye reflects the Gate Stone's pulsing glow in the background.
narrative: Selene opens her mouth. The first note is a breath. It splits the air. The Gate Stone hums. Kael's hands crack. Mira watches the futures begin to fray.
narration: Selene opens her mouth. The sound is not a sound. It is a breath that has waited three hundred years to escape. It carves the frigid air. Kael feels it vibrate through the litter, through his bones. The Gate Stone answers. A low hum climbs from the earth. Selene's silver eyes blaze. Her white hair lifts in a wind pulled from between worlds. The fungal robes billow. Kael’s knuckles split against the pine wood. He holds. Mira watches the threads shiver and burn. This note is a wound in the world. It is the sound of a gate unlocking. It is the price. Selene's ribcage expands, her throat trembles. She holds the pitch, clean and high and final. Kael smells ozone and blood. He does not look away from the Gate Stone, where light now bleeds from every seam, promising a future beyond this mountain.

title: The Gate Opens
prompt: A cracked black monolith erupts with blinding white light. The silhouette of a frail elder on a litter, a kneeling grandson, and a watching rift-walker are dwarfed against the rift as distant figures walk into the glow.
narrative: The song breaks. A silent scream rips the air. The Gate Stone splits. Ancient light spills across the valley. Selene falls back, silver eyes empty. Kael catches her. Her life has paid the toll. Fifty thousand souls begin their march. Mira watches the last thread settle. The futures are one.
narration: The final note dies in Selene’s throat. A crack, silent and bone-deep, splits the Gate Stone’s heart. White light floods the dead valley, smelling of rain and living soil. Her body slumps, a bundle of fungal robes and silver hair. Kael catches her. Three centuries of weight settle in his arms. Behind them, the shuffling begins. Fifty thousand feet move toward the light, a tide of silent hope. Mira closes her eyes. The eighth thread clicks into place. The only one. Kael holds his grandmother. The warmth she sang into being washes over them both.
